en: A common, round fruit produced by the tree Malus domestica, cultivated in temperate climates.

examples.en: I eat an apple every day.
The apple is red and sweet.
She put the apple in the basket.

usage: 可数名词，复数 apples；是最常见的水果类入门词。

en: Someone or something that is large in stature

examples.en: The elephant is very big.
This is a big red ball.
My dog is bigger than yours.

usage: 反义词 small；比较级 bigger，最高级 biggest。

en: A member of the class of animals Aves in the phylum Chordata, characterized by being warm-blooded, having feathers and wings usually capable of flight, and laying eggs.

examples.en: A small bird is singing in the tree.
The bird can fly high.
I see a blue bird near the window.
examples.cn: 我看见窗边有一只蓝色的鸟。

usage: 可数名词，复数 birds。

en: The colour of the clear sky or the deep sea, between green and violet in the visible spectrum, and one of the primary additive colours for transmitted light; the colour obtained by subtracting red and green from white light using magenta and cyan filters; or any colour resembling this.

examples.en: The sky is blue today.
She has a blue schoolbag.
I like the blue pen on the desk.
examples.cn: 我喜欢桌上那支蓝色的钢笔。

usage: 颜色词；也常形容「忧郁的」情绪。

en: A collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge, containing printed or written material, pictures, etc.

examples.en: I read a book before sleep.
This book is very interesting.
Please open your book to page ten.

usage: 可数名词，复数 books；动词 book 意为「预订」。

en: An animal of the family Felidae:

examples.en: The cat is sleeping on the chair.
My cat likes to play with a ball.
A black cat is in the garden.

usage: 可数名词，复数 cats；常见宠物类单词。

en: An end or conclusion.

examples.en: Please close the door.
Close your eyes and listen.
He closed the window at night.

usage: 反义词 open；形容词 closed（关着的）。

en: Coming, arrival; approach.

examples.en: Come here, little dog!
My friend will come to my home.
Please come with me.

usage: 反义词 go；过去式 came。

en: A mammal, Canis familiaris or Canis lupus familiaris, that has been domesticated for thousands of years, of highly variable appearance due to human breeding.

examples.en: The dog is running after a ball.
I have a small white dog.
The dog eats meat every day.

usage: 可数名词，复数 dogs；与 cat 相对。

en: Something to be eaten; a meal; a food item.

examples.en: We eat rice for lunch.
Don't eat too much candy.
The boy is eating an apple.

usage: 过去式 ate，过去分词 eaten。

en: A group of people who are closely related to one another (by blood, marriage or adoption); kin; for example, a set of parents and their children; an immediate family.

examples.en: I love my family.
We have a big family.
Family is very important.

usage: 作「家庭整体」时视为单数，如 My family is big.

en: A cold-blooded vertebrate animal that lives in water, moving with the help of fins and breathing with gills.

examples.en: The fish is swimming in the water.
I eat fish for dinner.
He caught a big fish.

usage: 单复同形（fish）；指不同种类时用 fishes。

en: A person other than a family member, spouse or lover whose company one enjoys and towards whom one feels affection.

examples.en: She is my good friend.
I play with my friend after school.
examples.cn: 放学后我和朋友一起玩。

examples.en: We are friends forever.

usage: 可数，复数 friends；形容词 friendly（友好的）。

en: The act of going.

examples.en: Let's go to school.
I go home at five.
They go to the park on Sunday.

usage: 过去式 went，过去分词 gone；go to school 去上学。

en: (of people)

examples.en: This is a good book.
He is a good student.
The food is very good.

usage: 反义词 bad；比较级 better，最高级 best。

en: Having green as its color.

examples.en: The grass is green.
She wears a green dress.
examples.cn: 她穿着一条绿色的裙子。

examples.en: I have a green apple.

usage: 颜色词；也指「环保的」，如 green energy。

en: A happy event, thing, person, etc.

examples.en: I am happy today.
The children are happy in the park.
examples.cn: 孩子们在公园里很开心。

examples.en: She is happy to see her friend.
examples.cn: 她很高兴见到她的朋友。

usage: 反义词 sad；名词 happiness（幸福）。

en: (stative) To perceive sounds through the ear.

examples.en: I hear a bird singing.
Can you hear the music?
She heard a loud noise.

usage: 过去式 heard；名词 hearing（听力）。

en: "Hello!" or an equivalent greeting.

examples.en: Hello! How are you?
He said hello to the teacher.
Say hello to your mother.

usage: 见面时的问候语；回应可用 hi / hello。

en: Action given to provide assistance; aid.

examples.en: Can you help me, please?
He helps his mother at home.
Thank you for your help.

usage: helpful（有用的）；can't help doing 忍不住做。

en: 看；看起来

examples.en: Look at the blackboard.
She looks happy today.
Look! A red bird!

usage: look at（看……）；look 强调「看」的动作。

en: Strong affection.

examples.en: I love my family.
She loves to read books.
We love our school.

usage: 可作动词也可作名词；lovely 意为「可爱的」。

en: The Earth's moon Luna; the sole natural satellite of the Earth, represented in astronomy and astrology by ☾.

examples.en: The moon is bright tonight.
We can see the moon in the sky.
examples.cn: 我们能看见天上的月亮。

examples.en: A star is next to the moon.

usage: 通常加 the：the moon。

en: Not closed

examples.en: Please open the window.
Open your book to page five.
He opened the door for me.

usage: 反义词 close；形容词 open（开着的）。

en: Activity for amusement only, especially among the young.

examples.en: The children play in the park.
I play football after school.
She plays the piano every day.

usage: play the piano（弹钢琴）中乐器前加 the。

en: A reading or an act of reading, especially an actor's part of a play.

examples.en: I read a story book at night.
Can you read this word?
He reads English every morning.

usage: 过去式 read 读作 /red/，过去分词 read。

en: Any of a range of colours having the longest wavelengths, 670 nm, of the visible spectrum; a primary additive colour for transmitted light: the colour obtained by subtracting green and blue from white light using magenta and yellow filters; the colour of blood, ripe strawberries, etc.

examples.en: I have a red apple.
She wears a red coat.
examples.cn: 她穿着一件红色的外套。

examples.en: The red flower is very beautiful.

usage: 表示颜色的形容词，常作定语修饰名词。

en: To run.

examples.en: The dog runs fast.
Don't run in the classroom.
He runs to school every morning.
examples.cn: 他每天早上跑步去上学。

usage: 不及物；过去式 ran，过去分词 run。

en: (collective) A group of fish or a group of marine mammals such as porpoises, dolphins, or whales.

examples.en: We go to school by bus.
My school is very big.
The teacher is at school.

usage: 常搭配 go to school（去上学，不加 the）。

en: (stative) To perceive or detect with the eyes, or as if by sight.

examples.en: I see a cat under the table.
examples.cn: 我看见桌子下面有一只猫。

examples.en: Can you see the moon?
I see a red bird in the tree.
examples.cn: 我看见树上有一只红色的鸟。

usage: 过去式 saw，过去分词 seen。

en: A gathering at which people sing songs.

examples.en: She likes to sing songs.
The bird sings in the morning.
We sing together in class.

usage: 过去式 sang；名词 singer 意为「歌手」。

en: Any part of something that is smaller or slimmer than the rest, now usually with anatomical reference to the back.

examples.en: This is a small cat.
I have a small red ball.
The small dog is very cute.

usage: 反义词 big；强调尺寸小。

en: Any small luminous dot appearing in the cloudless portion of the night sky, especially with a fixed location relative to other such dots.

examples.en: There are many stars in the sky.
I see a bright star.
examples.cn: 我看见一颗明亮的星星。

examples.en: The star is next to the moon.

usage: 可数，复数 stars；也指「明星」。

en: A person who studies or learns about a particular subject.

examples.en: He is a good student.
The students are in the classroom.
She is a new student here.

usage: 对应 teacher；pupil 多指小学生。

en: A star, especially when seen as the centre of any single solar system.

examples.en: The sun is hot in summer.
We can see the sun in the day.
The sun rises in the east.

usage: 世界上独一无二，通常加 the：the sun。

en: A person who teaches, especially one employed in a school.

examples.en: Our teacher is very kind.
The teacher helps the students.
I like my English teacher.

usage: 职业类名词；对应 student（学生）。

en: An expression of appreciation; a thought.

examples.en: Thank you very much!
He thanked his friend.
No, thank you.

usage: thank you = 谢谢你；形容词 thankful（感激的）。

en: Fast growing function based on Kruskal's tree theorem.

examples.en: A bird is in the tree.
The tree is very tall.
We plant a small tree.

usage: 可数名词，复数 trees。

en: A substance (of molecular formula H₂O) found at room temperature and pressure as a clear liquid; it is present naturally as rain, and found in rivers, lakes and seas; its solid form is ice and its gaseous form is steam.

examples.en: I drink water every day.
The fish lives in the water.
Please give me a glass of water.

usage: 不可数名词，不能说 a water；表「水域/海水」时可用复数 waters。

en: The act or style of writing.

examples.en: I write a letter to my friend.
She writes her name on the book.
Can you write in English?

usage: 名词 writer 意为「作家」；过去式 wrote。

en: The colour of gold, butter, or a lemon; the colour obtained by mixing green and red light, or by subtracting blue from white light.

examples.en: The sun is yellow.
She has a yellow schoolbag.
I see a yellow flower.

usage: 颜色词；也可指「胆怯的」。

en: Easy to use.

examples.en: I’ll see you as soon as I’m able.
After the past week of forced marches, only half the men are fully able.
He was born to an able family.
The chairman was also an able sailor.

usage: be able to do 表示「能够」，相当于 can 的多种时态形式。

en: The state or quality of being active; activeness.

examples.en: Pit row was abuzz with activity.
The activity for the morning was a walk to the store.
An increasing number of sports activities are on offer at the university.

usage: 可数名词，复数 activities；形容词 active（活跃的）。

en: Another time; once more.

examples.en: Great, thanks again!
Bring us word again.
The South will rise again.

usage: 表重复，常置于句末或动词之后。

en: Prior to some specified time, either past, present, or future; by this time; previously.

examples.en: Are you quitting already?

usage: 多用于肯定句完成时；否定/疑问常用 yet。

en: To fill with wonder and surprise; to astonish, astound, surprise or perplex.

examples.en: He was amazed when he found that the girl was a robot.

2015, June 10, Lindsey Bever, "Morning Mix: Another reason seeing-eye dogs are amazing"http//www.washingtonpost.com/news/morning-mix/wp/2015/06/10/another-heroic-story-showing-why-seeing-eye-dogs-are-amazing/

usage: 由动词 amaze 变来；修饰物；修饰人用 amazed。

en: A person who is very old.

examples.en: an ancient city  an ancient forest
an ancient author  an ancient empire

usage: 反义词 modern（现代的）。

en: An additional one of the same kind.

examples.en: There is one sterling and here is another
I saw one movie, but I think I will see another.
His interests keep shifting from one thing to another.

usage: an + other，后接单数名词；泛指「另一个」。

en: To come or be in sight; to be in view; to become visible.

examples.en: A great writer appeared at that time.
He appeared quite happy with the result.

usage: 反义词 disappear（消失）；作系动词表「似乎」。

en: To show grounds for concluding (that); to indicate, imply.

examples.en: He also argued for stronger methods to be used against China.
He argued his point.

usage: argue with sb. 与某人争论；名词 argument。

en: (with the verb "to be") Present in the vicinity.

examples.en: Is Clare around today?
"How is old Bob? I heard that his health is failing."  "Oh, he's still around. He's feeling better now."
High above, vultures circled around.
Everybody please gather around.

usage: 表地点「围绕」或表时间/数量「大约」。

en: A person who creates art.
usage: 由 art（艺术）派生；artistic 艺术的。

en: A necessary commodity, a staple requirement.

examples.en: Rice is a basic for many Asian villagers.
Arithmetic is a basic for the study of mathematics.
The drill sergeants gave him hell in basic.
Flour is a basic ingredient of bread.

usage: 名词 base（基础）或 basis；反义词 advanced。

en: Human conduct relative to social norms.

examples.en: He was on his best behavior when her family visited.

usage: 不可数名词；形容词 behavioral（行为的）。

en: The physique of a human body; constitution or structure of a human body.

examples.en: Rugby players are of sturdy build.
The computer company has introduced a new prototype build to beta testers.
I made a build that looked like the Parthenon in that game.
This code won't build any more. Have you made any changes?

usage: 过去式/分词 built；名词 building（建筑物）。

en: A period of 100 consecutive years; often specifically a numbered period with conventional start and end dates, e.g., the twentieth century, which stretches from (strictly) 1901 through 2000, or (informally) 1900 through 1999. The first century AD was from 1 to 100.

examples.en: That was his tenth professional century.

usage: 100 年为一世纪；复数 centuries。

en: (with "the") Something certain.

examples.en: I was certain of my decision.
Bankruptcy is the certain outcome of your constant gambling and lending.
There were serious objections to certain of the proposals.

usage: be certain of/about 确信；a certain 某一（不具体）。

en: An area of the earth's surface between two parallels of latitude.

examples.en: Industries that require a lot of fossil fuels are unlikely to be popular in the current political climate.

usage: 区别 weather（天气，短时）；climate 指长期气候。

en: To gather together; amass.

examples.en: Suzanne collected all the papers she had laid out.
A bank collects a monthly payment on a client's new car loan.   A mortgage company collects a monthly payment on a house.
I don't think he collects as much as hoards.
He had a lot of trouble collecting on that bet he made.

usage: collect stamps 集邮；名词 collection。

en: To impart
usage: communicate with sb.；名词 communication。

en: Comparison.

examples.en: Compare the tiger's coloration with that of the zebra.
Astronomers have compared comets to dirty snowballs.
We compare "good" as "good", "better", "best".
A sapling and a fully-grown oak tree do not compare.

usage: compare A with B 比较；compare A to B 把 A 比作 B。

en: A completed survey.

examples.en: He completed the assignment on time.
The last chapter completes the book nicely.
After she found the rook, the chess set was complete.
When your homework is complete, you can go and play with Martin.

usage: 作动词「完成」；作形容词「彻底的」，反义词 incomplete。

en: To think about seriously.

examples.en: Consider that we’ve had three major events and the year has hardly begun.
I’m considering going to the beach tomorrow.
Consider yourself lucky, but consider your opponent skillful.
She sat there for a moment, considering him.

usage: consider doing 考虑做；consider sb. (as) 认为。

en: An option allowing a gamer to resume play after game over, when all lives have been lost.

examples.en: Do you want me to continue to unload these?
When will the concert continue?
This meeting has been continued to the thirteenth of July.

usage: continue doing/to do 继续做；名词 continuation。

en: Influence or authority over something.

examples.en: The government has complete control over the situation.
With a simple remote, he could control the toy truck.

usage: under control 受控；out of control 失控。

en: Free from error; true; accurate.
usage: 作形容词「对的」，反义词 wrong；作动词「纠正」。

en: To bring into existence; (sometimes in particular:)

examples.en: You can create the color orange by mixing yellow and red.
A sudden chemical spill on the highway created a chain‐collision which created a record traffic jam.
Henry VIII created him a Duke.   Last month, the queen created two barons.
Children usually enjoy creating, never mind if it is of any use!

usage: 名词 creation；creative 有创造力的。

en: The arts, customs, lifestyles, background, and habits that characterize a particular society or nation.

examples.en: I'm headed to the lab to make sure my cell culture hasn't died.

usage: 形容词 cultural（文化的）。

en: To resolve (a contest, problem, dispute, etc.); to choose, determine, or settle

examples.en: Her last-minute goal decided the game.
I have decided that it is healthier to walk to work.

usage: decide to do 决定做；名词 decision。

en: To change with a specific direction, progress.

examples.en: Let's see how things develop and then make our decision.
Isabel developed from a tropical depression to a tropical storm to a hurricane.   An embryo develops into a fetus and then into an infant.
I need to develop a plan for the next three weeks.
Please develop this roll of film.

usage: develop a habit 养成习惯；名词 development。

en: To find or learn something for the first time.

examples.en: Turning the corner, I discovered a lovely little shop. I discovered that they sold widgets.
The gust of wind discovered a bone in the sand.
This move discovers an attack on a vital pawn.
I discovered my plans to the rest of the team.

usage: 指发现原有事物；名词 discovery，反义词 invent（发明）。

en: To converse or debate concerning a particular topic.

examples.en: I don't wish to discuss this further. Let's talk about something else.

usage: discuss sth. with sb.；名词 discussion。

en: The surroundings of, and influences on, a particular item of interest.

examples.en: That program uses the Microsoft Windows environment.

usage: 形容词 environmental（环境的）；protect the environment 环保。

en: To make plain, manifest, or intelligible; to clear of obscurity; to illustrate the meaning of.

examples.en: To explain a chapter of the Bible.

usage: explain sth. to sb.；名词 explanation。

en: To make famous; to bring renown to.

examples.en: Some people are only famous within their city.

usage: be famous for 因……著名；be famous as 作为……著名。

en: (sometimes attributive) In billiards and similar games, a stroke causing a ball to follow another ball after hitting it.

examples.en: a follow shot
Follow that car!
B follows A in the alphabet.
Follow these instructions to the letter.

usage: follow the rules 遵守规则；反义词 lead（领导）。

en: A foreign person, particularly:

examples.en: He liked visiting foreign cities.
There are many more foreign students in Europe since the Erasmus scheme started.
foreign policy; foreign navies
foreign body; foreign substance; foreign gene; foreign species

usage: foreign language 外语；foreigner 外国人。

en: To lose remembrance of.

examples.en: I have forgotten most of the things I learned in school.
I forgot to buy flowers for my wife at our 14th wedding anniversary.
I forgot my car keys.
Let's just forget about it.

usage: 反义词 remember；过去式 forgot，过去分词 forgotten。

en: A plait or fold in cloth, made by drawing a thread through it; a pucker.

examples.en: I've been gathering ideas from the people I work with.
She gathered the shawl about her as she stepped into the cold.
From his silence, I gathered that things had not gone well.
Salt water can help boils to gather and then burst.

usage: gather information 收集信息；反义词 scatter（散开）。

en: To reach a partly (or totally) unqualified conclusion.

examples.en: He who guesses the riddle shall have the ring.
That album is quite hard to find, but I guess you could try ordering it online.
"I guess I'll go to bed."

usage: make a guess 猜一下；I guess 我想/大概。

en: An action performed on a regular basis.

examples.en: It’s become a habit of mine to have a cup of coffee after dinner.
By force of habit, he dressed for work even though it was holiday.
It’s interesting how Catholic and Buddhist monks both wear habits.
The new riding habits of the team looked smashing!

usage: form a habit 养成习惯；good/bad habit 好/坏习惯。

en: To occur or take place.

examples.en: Let me tell you how it happened.
Do you happen to have an umbrella?

usage: 不及物，无被动；sth. happened to sb. 某人出了某事。

en: Enjoying health and vigor of body, mind, or spirit: well.

examples.en: He was father to three healthy kids.
A healthy diet and exercise can help to maintain proper weight.
Sam unwrapped the sandwich and took a healthy bite out of the middle.

usage: 名词 health（健康）；keep healthy 保持健康。

en: To make (something) better; to increase the value or productivity (of something).

examples.en: Buying more servers would improve performance.
I have improved since taking the tablets.
to improve negligence
to improve one's time;  to improve his means

usage: improvement 进步；反义词 worsen（恶化）。

en: A piece of source code or other content that is dynamically retrieved for inclusion in another item.

examples.en: I will purchase the vacation package if you will include car rental.
Does this volume of Shakespeare include his sonnets?
You have to include the strings library to use this function.

usage: including 包括；反义词 exclude（排除）。

en: An amount by which a quantity is increased.

examples.en: His rage only increased when I told him of the lost money.
The Moon increases.

usage: 反义词 decrease（减少）；on the increase 在增长。

en: (of people) To cause (someone) to be acquainted (with someone else).

examples.en: Let me introduce you to my friends.
Let me introduce our guest speaker.
Various pollutants were introduced into the atmosphere.
Wheeled transport was introduced long ago.

usage: introduce A to B 把 A 介绍给 B；名词 introduction。

en: To ask for the presence or participation of someone or something.

examples.en: We invited our friends round for dinner.
I invite you all to be seated.
I always invite criticism of my definitions.

usage: invite sb. to 邀请某人去；名词 invitation。

en: The fact of knowing about something; general understanding or familiarity with a subject, place, situation etc.

examples.en: His knowledge of Iceland was limited to what he'd seen on the Travel Channel.
Knowledge consists in recognizing the difference between good and bad decisions.
A secretary should have a good knowledge of shorthand.
His library contained the accumulated knowledge of the Greeks and Romans.

usage: 不可数名词；形容词 knowledgeable（博学的）。

en: A body of words, and set of methods of combining them (called a grammar), understood by a community and used as a form of communication.

examples.en: Deaf and mute people communicate using languages like ASL.
the gift of language
legal language;   the language of chemistry
body language;   the language of the eyes

usage: 可数名词；mother tongue 母语。

en: The act of learning something

examples.en: learn from one's mistakes
I learn medicine.
He just learned that he will be sacked.

usage: 反义词 teach（教）；learn from 向……学习。

en: A speaking or notice of anything, usually in a brief or cursory manner. Used especially in the phrase make mention of.
usage: Don't mention it. 别客气（回应感谢）。

en: Someone who lives in modern times.

examples.en: Our online interactive game is a modern approach to teaching about gum disease.  Although it was built in the 1600s, the building still has a very modern look.

usage: 反义词 ancient（古老的）。

en: A native inhabitant of a place, country etc.

examples.en: He's a natural on the saxophone.
The species will be under threat if its natural habitat is destroyed.
In the natural world the fit tend to live on while the weak perish.
Natural food is healthier than processed food.

usage: 名词 nature（自然）；反义词 artificial（人造的）。

en: New information of interest.

examples.en: Is there any news about the storm?
Did you hear/read/see the latest news?

usage: 不可数名词，谓语用单数；a piece of news 一条新闻。

en: A proposal that has been made.

examples.en: I decline your offer to contract.
His offer was $3.50 per share.
His first letter was not a real offer, but an attempt to determine interest.

usage: offer to do 主动提出做；offer sb. sth. 向某人提供。

en: A subjective belief, judgment or perspective that a person has formed about a topic, issue, person or thing.

examples.en: Every man is a fool in some man's opinion.

usage: in my opinion 我认为；have an opinion on 对……有看法。

en: A substance that is applied as a liquid or paste, and dries into a solid coating that protects or adds color/colour to an object or surface to which it has been applied.

examples.en: The Nimrods are strong on the outside, but not very good in the paint.
I am running low on paint for my marker.
to paint a portrait or a landscape
I've been painting since I was a young child.

usage: 名词 painter（画家）；painting（画作）。

en: A person or animal who receives treatment from a doctor or other medically educated person.

examples.en: The subject of a passive verb is usually a patient.
Be patient: your friends will arrive in a few hours.
patient endeavour

usage: 反义词 impatient；名词 patience（耐心）。

en: To do something; to execute.

examples.en: It took him only twenty minutes to perform the task.
She will perform in the play.

usage: performance 表演/表现；perform well 表现出色。

en: A drawing showing technical details of a building, machine, etc., with unwanted details omitted, and often using symbols rather than detailed drawing to represent doors, valves, etc.

examples.en: The plans for many important buildings were once publicly available.
He didn't really have a plan; he had a goal and a habit of control.
Seen in plan, the building had numerous passageways not apparent to visitors.
a phone plan

usage: plan to do 计划做；make a plan 制定计划。

en: An organism that is not an animal, especially an organism capable of photosynthesis. Typically a small or herbaceous organism of this kind, rather than a tree.

examples.en: The garden had a couple of trees, and a cluster of colourful plants around the border.
That gun's not mine! It's a plant! I've never seen it before!
That gun's not mine! It was planted there by the real murderer!
Plant your feet firmly and give the rope a good tug.

usage: 反义词 animal（动物）；plant trees 植树。

en: A person who is popular, especially at a school.
usage: be popular with 受……欢迎；反义词 unpopular。

en: Repetition of an activity to improve a skill.

examples.en: He will need lots of practice with the lines before he performs them.
Being on a team is hard: you're always having to go to practice while everyone else is taking it easy.
She ran a thriving medical practice.
It is good practice to check each door and window before leaving.

usage: practice doing 练习做；美 practise 作动词，practice 作名词。

en: Preparation

examples.en: We prepared the spacecraft for takeoff.
We prepared a fish for dinner.
We prepared for a bumpy ride.
She prepared a meal from what was left in the cupboards.

usage: prepare for 为……准备；preparation 准备。

en: To stop (an outcome); to keep from (doing something).

examples.en: I brush my teeth regularly to prevent them from turning yellow.

usage: prevent...from doing 阻止……做；名词 prevention。

en: Feeling honoured (by something); feeling happy or satisfied about an event or fact; gratified.

examples.en: I am proud of Sivu’s schoolwork.
I was too proud to apologise.
It was a proud day when we finally won the championship.
After it had healed, the scar tissue stood proud of his flesh.

usage: be proud of 以……为傲；名词 pride。

en: The absence of sound; quietness.

examples.en: There was a strange quiet in the normally very lively plaza.
When you quiet, we can start talking.
Can you quiet your child? He's making lots of noise.
I can't hear the music; it is too quiet.

usage: 反义词 noisy（吵闹的）；keep quiet 保持安静。

en: To make real; to convert from the imaginary or fictitious into reality; to bring into real existence

examples.en: The objectives of the project were never fully realized.
He realized that he had left his umbrella on the train.
to realize large profits from a speculation
By realizing the company's assets, the liquidator was able to return most of the shareholders' investments.

usage: realize a dream 实现梦想；名词 realization。

en: An operation in which data is received.

examples.en: She received many presents for her birthday.
to receive a lodger, visitor, ambassador, messenger, etc.
I received a bloody nose from the collision.

usage: 反义词 send（寄）；名词 receipt（收据）。

en: (chiefly in the plural) That which is left; relic; remainder.

examples.en: There was no food in the house, so I had to remain hungry.
The light remained red for two full minutes.

usage: 作系动词「保持」，后接形容词；remain silent 保持沉默。

en: To recall from one's memory; to have an image in one's memory.

examples.en: Please remember this formula!
Remember what I've said.
Remember to lock the door when you go out.
Please remember me to your brother.

usage: 反义词 forget；remember to do 记得去做。

en: To ask (someone) for something; to request.
usage: require doing/to do；require sth. of sb. 对某人要求。

en: Diligent inquiry or examination to seek or revise facts, principles, theories, applications, etc.; laborious or continued search after truth.

examples.en: The research station that houses Wang and his team is outside Lijiang, a city of about 1.2 million people.

usage: 不可数；do research on 对……做研究。

en: The act of returning.

examples.en: I expect the house to be spotless upon my return.
Do you want a one-way or a return?
Last year there were 250 returns of this product, an improvement on the 500 returns the year before.
a return to one's question

usage: return to 回到；return sth. to 把……还给。

en: A box, usually made of metal, in which valuables can be locked for safekeeping.

examples.en: You’ll be safe here.
It’s safe to eat this.
We have to find a safe spot, where we can hide out until this is over.
The pitcher attempted to pick off the runner at first, but he was safe.

usage: 反义词 dangerous；名词 safety（安全）。

en: An attempt to find something.

examples.en: With only five minutes until we were meant to leave, the search for the keys started in earnest.
Search is a hard problem for computers to solve efficiently.
I searched the garden for the keys and found them in the vegetable patch.
The police are searching for evidence in his flat.

usage: search for 寻找；search engine 搜索引擎。

en: An act of putting the ball or shuttlecock in play in various games.

examples.en: Whose serve is it?
I've received a summons for jury duty. It says I serve one day or one trial.

usage: service 服务（名词）；serve as 充当。

en: An area of land in private ownership (as opposed to common land).
usage: 后接复数可数名词，比 some 更明确数量。

en: A portion of something, especially a portion given or allotted to someone.

examples.en: Upload media from the browser or directly to the file share.
to share a shelter with another;  They share a language.
He shared his story with the press.

usage: share sth. with sb. 与某人分享；share the cost 分摊费用。

en: That which is similar to, or resembles, something else, as in quality, form, etc.
usage: be similar to 与……相似；反义词 different。

en: A long-standing group of people sharing cultural aspects such as language, dress, norms of behavior and artistic forms.

examples.en: This society has been known for centuries for its colorful clothing and tight-knit family structure.
It was then that they decided to found a society of didgeridoo-playing unicyclists.
The gap between Western and Eastern societies seems to be narrowing.
Our global society develops in fits and starts.

usage: 形容词 social（社会的）；modern society 现代社会。

en: (chiefly law enforcement) A solution; an explanation.
usage: solve a problem 解决问题；名词 solution。

en: Amount of money spent (during a period); expenditure.

examples.en: I’m sorry, boss, but the advertising spend exceeded the budget again this month.
He spends far more on gambling than he does on living proper.
to spend an estate in gambling
The violence of the waves was spent.

usage: sb. spend(s) time/money on sth./(in) doing 人花费。

en: Vagina

examples.en: He thought it strange that his girlfriend wore shorts in the winter.
I moved to a strange town when I was ten.

usage: 反义词 familiar（熟悉的）；名词 stranger（陌生人）。

en: To follow in order; to come next after; hence, to take the place of.

examples.en: Autumn succeeds summer.
The persecution of any righteous practice has never succeeded in the face of history; in fact, it can expedite the collapse of the persecutory regime.
So, if the issue of the elder son succeed before the younger, I am king.

usage: succeed in doing 成功做成；名词 success，反义词 fail。

en: To imply but stop short of saying explicitly.

examples.en: Are you suggesting that I killed my wife?
The name "hamburger" suggests that hamburgers originated from Hamburg.
I’d like to suggest that we go out to lunch.   I’d like to suggest going out to lunch.

usage: suggest doing（不接 to do）；名词 suggestion。

en: (sometimes attributive) Something which supports.

examples.en: Don't move that beam! It's a support for the whole platform.
The government provides support to the arts in several ways.
Sure they sell the product, but do they provide support?
If the membership function of a fuzzy set is continuous, then that fuzzy set's support is an open set.

usage: 反义词 oppose（反对）；名词 support。

en: The overside or up-side of a flat object such as a table, or of a liquid.

examples.en: On the surface, the spy looked like a typical businessman.

usage: 反义词 bottom（底部）；on the surface 表面上。

en: Something unexpected.

examples.en: It was a surprise to find out I owed twice as much as I thought I did.
Imagine my surprise on learning I owed twice as much as I thought I did.
It surprises me that I owe twice as much as I thought I did.
He doesn’t know that I’m in the country – I thought I’d turn up at his house and surprise him.

usage: to one's surprise 令某人惊讶的是；surprised/surprising。

en: The organization of knowledge for practical purposes.
usage: 形容词 technical（技术的）；high technology 高科技。

en: A measure of cold or heat, often measurable with a thermometer.

examples.en: The temperature in the room dropped nearly 20 degrees; it went from hot to cold.
You have a temperature. I think you should stay home today. You’re sick.

usage: take one's temperature 量体温；high/low temperature 高/低温。

en: Elongated portion.

examples.en: She trained seven hours a day to prepare for the Olympics.
You can't train a pig to write poetry.
I trained with weights all winter.
The assassin had trained his gun on the minister.

usage: 作动词「训练」；trainer 教练；training 训练。

en: In Euclidean spaces: a set of points obtained by adding a given fixed vector to each point of a given set.

examples.en: William was translated by the blow to the head he received, being unable to speak for the next few minutes.

usage: translate A into B 把 A 译成 B；translation 翻译。

en: The act of traveling; passage from place to place.

examples.en: space travel
I’m off on my travels around France again.
My drill press has a travel of only 1.5 inches.
Soundwaves can travel through water.

usage: 名词 traveler（旅行者）；travel abroad 出国旅行。

en: Confidence in or reliance on some person or quality.

examples.en: He needs to regain her trust if he is ever going to win her back.
I was out of cash, but the landlady let me have it on trust.
I put the house into my sister's trust.
We cannot trust anyone who deceives us.

usage: trust sb. 信任某人；反义词 distrust。

en: Not able; lacking a certain ability.

examples.en: Are you unable to mind your own business or something?

usage: be unable to do 不能做；反义词 able。

en: Institution of higher education (typically accepting students from the age of about 17 or 18, depending on country, but in some exceptional cases able to take younger students) where subjects are studied and researched in depth and degrees are offered.

examples.en: The only reason why I haven't gone to university is because I can't afford it.

usage: 可数，注意 a university（u 发辅音 /j/）。

en: The typical state of something, or something that is typical.

examples.en: I'll just have the usual.
It is becoming more usual these days to rear children as bilingual.

usage: as usual 像往常一样；反义词 unusual。

en: The quality (positive or negative) that renders something desirable or valuable.

examples.en: The Shakespearean Shylock is of dubious value in the modern world.
The value of my children's happiness is second only to that of my wife.
He does not share his parents' values.
The value of a crotchet is twice that of a quaver.

usage: 形容词 valuable（有价值的）；反义词 valueless。

en: Having a broad range (of different elements).

examples.en: The reasons are various.
a various reading of a Biblical text

usage: 由 vary（变化）派生；后接复数名词。

en: (physical) Matter, material.

examples.en: He's working out with weights.
the weight of care or business
He was pushing weight.

usage: 形容词 heavy（重的）；lose/gain weight 减/增重。

en: A ball that passes so far from the batsman that the umpire deems it unplayable; the arm signal used by an umpire to signal a wide; the extra run added to the batting side's score

examples.en: We walked down a wide corridor.
The inquiry had a wide remit.
That team needs a decent wide player.
Too bad! That was a great passing-shot, but it's wide.

usage: 反义词 narrow；副词 widely（广泛地）。

en: To conquer, defeat.

examples.en: to win the jackpot in a lottery;  to win a bottle of wine in a raffle
Who would win in a fight between an octopus and a dolphin?
Ever since the 2013 Boston Marathon bombing, Bostonians now run as "One Boston." The terrorists did not win.
The company hopes to win an order from the government worth over 5 million dollars.

usage: 反义词 lose；过去式 won；win a prize 获奖。

en: To include so that it no longer has separate existence; to overwhelm; to cause to disappear as if by swallowing up; to incorporate; to assimilate; to take in and use up.

examples.en: Heat, light, and electricity are absorbed in the substances into which they pass.

usage: be absorbed in 专心致志于；absorbed 专注的。

en: Accusation.

examples.en: For the U.S. President to be impeached, he must be accused of a high crime or misdemeanor.

usage: accuse sb. of (doing) 指控某人（做）……。

en: To make suitable; to make to correspond; to fit or suit

examples.en: to adapt a story for the stage
a word of an adapted form
They could not adapt to the new climate and so perished.

usage: adapt to 适应；adapt...for 将……改编为。

en: A situation which allows a mutually exclusive choice between two or more possibilities; a choice between two or more possibilities.

examples.en: an alternative proposition

usage: have no alternative but to 别无选择只能；alternative energy 新能源。

en: To subject to analysis.
usage: 名词 analysis（分析）；analytical 分析的。

en: To gain (an object or desired result).

examples.en: To attain such a high level of proficiency requires hours of practice each day.

usage: attain one's goal 达成目标；近义词 achieve。

en: Something that is beneficial.

examples.en: Recycling and reusing garbage can be beneficial to the environment.

usage: be beneficial to 对……有益；名词 benefit（益处）。

en: That which attends, or relates to, or in some way affects, a fact or event; an attendant thing or state of things.

examples.cn: 在这种情况之下只能让步。

usage: under the circumstances 在此情况下；复数表「境况」。

en: A spoken or written remark.

examples.en: I have no comment on that.
I wish I'd commented this complicated algorithm back when I remembered how it worked.

usage: comment on 对……评论；No comment. 无可奉告。

en: The act of committing (e.g. a database transaction or source code into a source control repository), making it a permanent change.

examples.en: Tony should be committed to a nuthouse!
to commit a series of heinous crimes
to commit oneself to a certain action

usage: commit to 致力于；commit a crime 犯罪；committed 坚定的。

en: A network of interconnected systems.

examples.en: Jim has a real complex about working for a woman boss.

usage: 反义词 simple；complexity 复杂性。

en: To include, comprise; to contain.

examples.cn: 我无法完全理解这篇文章。

usage: comprehension 理解力；comprehensive 全面的。

en: A substance that is in a condensed form.

examples.en: to concentrate rays of light into a focus
to concentrate acid by evaporation
Population tends to concentrate in cities.
Let me concentrate!

usage: concentrate on 专心于；concentration 专注。

en: To end; to come to an end.

examples.en: The story concluded with a moral.
to conclude a bargain
From the evidence, I conclude that this man was murdered.
A judgment concludes the introduction of further evidence.

usage: conclude that 断定；conclusion 结论。

en: The act or method of controlling or directing

examples.en: Good conduct will be rewarded and likewise poor conduct will be punished.
The commander conducted thousands of troops.
He conducted himself well.

usage: conduct a survey 进行调查；conductor 导体/指挥。

en: That which follows something on which it depends; that which is produced by a cause.

examples.en: I'm warning you. If you don't get me the report on time, there will be consequences.

usage: as a consequence 因此；consequent 随之发生的。

en: To think about seriously.

examples.en: Consider that we’ve had three major events and the year has hardly begun.
I’m considering going to the beach tomorrow.
Consider yourself lucky, but consider your opponent skillful.
She sat there for a moment, considering him.

usage: considerate of sb. 体谅某人；consider 考虑（动词）。

en: To use up.

examples.en: The power plant consumes 30 tons of coal per hour.
Baby birds consume their own weight in food each day.
Desire consumed him.
The building was consumed by fire.

usage: consumer 消费者；consumption 消耗量。

en: To make someone believe, or feel sure about something, especially by using logic, argument or evidence.

examples.en: I wouldn't have or do something, unless I'm convinced that it's good.

usage: convince sb. of/that 使某人相信；convincing 令人信服的。

en: Essential or decisive for determining the outcome or future of something; extremely important.

examples.en: A secure supply of crude oil is crucial for any modern nation, let alone a superpower.
Delbert Wilkins is the most crucial pirate radio DJ in Brixton.

usage: be crucial to/for 对……至关重要；近义词 vital。

en: To make clear, explain, interpret.

examples.en: He declared him innocent.
declare bankruptcy
Houghton and Sunderland South was the first constituency to declare in the 2015 general election.
The counter "i" was declared as an integer.

usage: declare war 宣战；declaration 宣言/申报。

en: Downward movement, fall.

examples.en: The dollar has declined rapidly since 2001.
My health declined in winter.
a line that declines from straightness
On reflection I think I will decline your generous offer.

usage: 作「拒绝」较正式；反义词 increase/rise。

en: To consider carefully; to weigh well in the mind.

examples.en: It is now time for the jury to deliberate the guilt of the defendant.
Tripping me was deliberate action.
The jury took eight hours to come to its deliberate verdict.
a deliberate opinion; a deliberate measure or result

usage: 反义词 accidental（偶然的）；deliberately 故意地。

en: To show how to use (something).

examples.en: Can you demonstrate the new tools for us?
Those people outside are demonstrating against the election results.

usage: demonstrate how 演示如何；demonstration 演示/示威。

en: To recognize someone or something as different from others based on its characteristics.

examples.en: The soldier distinguished himself in combat and received a medal.

usage: distinguish A from B 区分；distinguished 卓越的。

en: To divide into portions and dispense.

examples.en: He distributed the bread amongst his followers.
The agency distributes newspapers to local shops.
A network of children distributes flyers to every house.
I raked the soil then distributed grass seed.

usage: distribute sth. to 分发；distribution 分配/分销。

en: Ruling; governing; prevailing

examples.en: The dominant party controlled the government.
The dominant plants of the Carboniferous were lycopods and early conifers.

usage: dominant 主导的；domination 支配。

en: To completely remove, get rid of, put an end to.

examples.en: a ruthless mobster who eliminated his enemies
Bill was eliminated as a suspect when the police interviewed witnesses.

usage: eliminate poverty 消除贫困；elimination 消除。

en: To stress, give emphasis or extra weight to (something).

examples.en: His two-fingered gesture emphasized what he had told his boss to do with his job.

usage: emphasis 重点；place emphasis on 重视。

en: To make somebody able (to do, or to be, something); to give sufficient ability or power to do or to be; to give strength or ability to.

examples.en: April 16, 2018, Norimitsu Onishi and Selam Gebrekidan writing in The New York Times, ‘They Eat Money’: How Mandela’s Political Heirs Grow Rich Off Corruption
His parents enabled him to go on buying drugs.

usage: enable sb. to do 使某人能够做；反义词 disable。

en: To lift, raise up.

examples.en: A debt enhances rapidly by compound interest.

usage: enhance one's ability 提升能力；反义词 reduce。

en: To draw conclusions from examining; to assess.

examples.en: It will take several years to evaluate the material gathered in the survey.
Evaluate this integral.

usage: evaluate the effect 评估效果；evaluation 评估。

en: Obviously true by simple observation.

examples.en: It was evident she was angry, after she slammed the door.

usage: It is evident that 显然；evidently 显然地。

en: To be larger, greater than (something).

examples.en: The company's 2005 revenue exceeds that of 2004.
The quality of her essay has exceeded my expectations.
Your password cannot exceed eight characters.

usage: exceed the limit 超限；excess 过量（名词）。

en: To change (something) from a smaller form and/or size to a larger one; to spread out or lay open.

examples.en: You can expand this compact umbrella to cover a large table.
A flower expands its leaves.
Use the binomial theorem to expand {(x+1)}^4.
The expression {(x+1)}^4 expands to x^4 + 4x^3 + 6x^2 + 4x + 1.

usage: 反义词 shrink；expansion 扩张。

en: To reveal, uncover, make visible, bring to light, introduce to.

examples.cn: 别让孩子暴露于阳光下。

usage: expose...to 使……暴露于；exposure 暴露。

en: Previous.

examples.en: A former president;  the former East Germany
I drive two vehicles, a Chevy Camaro and an AMC Gremlin. I won the former on a game show.

usage: the former...the latter 前者……后者；反义词 latter。

en: (usually in the plural) A leading or primary principle, rule, law, or article, which serves as the groundwork of a system; an essential part

examples.en: one of the fundamentals of linear algebra
A need for belonging seems fundamental to humans.

usage: fundamentally 根本上；近义词 basic/essential。

en: (sometimes figurative) Grip.

examples.en: The goal is within my grasp.
I have never been able to grasp the concept of infinity.

usage: grasp the meaning 领会含义；grasp at 试图抓住。

en: Anything that assures a certain outcome.

examples.en: Can you give me a guarantee that he will be fit for the match?
The cooker comes with a five-year guarantee.
The long sunny days guarantee a good crop.

usage: guarantee to do 保证做；名词 guarantee 保修。

en: To establish the identity of someone or something.

examples.en: The Associated Press will not identify the suspect of the crime because he is a juvenile.

usage: identify with 认同；identification 识别/身份证。

en: Happening right away, instantly, with no delay.

examples.en: Computer users these days expect immediate results when they click on a link.
immediate family;  immediate vicinity
Bravo Three, this Bravo Six. Immediate! We are coming under fire from the north from an unknown enemy, over!
Hotel Two-Niner, this is Bravo Six. Immediate suppression at grid November-Kilo four-five-three two-one-five. Danger Close. I authenticate Golf Echo, over.

usage: immediately 立刻；an immediate response 即时回应。

en: To point out; to discover; to direct to a knowledge of; to show; to make known.

examples.en: The guard blew his whistle to indicate imminent departure.
Great prostration of strength indicates the use of stimulants.

usage: indication 迹象；indicator 指标。

en: (with on or upon or (that + ordinary verb form)) To hold up a claim emphatically.

examples.en: I insist that my secretary dresses nicely.
I insist that my secretary dress nicely.

usage: insist on doing 坚持做；insist that + 虚拟语气。

en: (usually followed by the particle "to") To hope; to wish (something, or something to be accomplished); be intent upon

examples.en: He intends to go to university.

usage: intend to do 打算做；intention 意图。

en: To explain or tell the meaning of; to translate orally into intelligible or familiar language or terms. applied especially to language, but also to dreams, signs, conduct, mysteries, etc.

examples.en: to interpret an Indian speech
The actor interpreted the character of Hamlet with great skill.
He interpreted at the meeting between the Chinese and French associates.

usage: interpreter 口译员；interpretation 解释/阐释。

en: To roll or fold up; to wind round; to entwine.

examples.en: to involve in darkness or obscurity
He's involved in the crime.
By getting involved in her local community, Mary met lots of people and also helped make it a nicer place to live.
a quantity involved to the third or fourth power

usage: involve doing 需要（做）；be involved in 参与。

en: To provide an acceptable explanation for.

examples.en: How can you justify spending so much money on clothes?
Nothing can justify your rude behaviour last night.
The text will look better justified.
She felt no need to justify herself for deciding not to invite him.

usage: justify doing 证明做……合理；justification 理由。

en: To support (someone), to back up or assist (someone) in an action.
usage: maintain a habit 保持习惯；maintenance 维护。

en: To confer with others in order to come to terms or reach an agreement.

examples.en: The client and server computers must first negotiate a network protocol to be used.
Although the car was quite rickety, he could negotiate the curves very well.

usage: negotiate with 与……谈判；negotiation 谈判。

en: To get hold of; to gain possession of, to procure; to acquire, in any way.
usage: 较正式「得到」；近义词 get/acquire；反义词 lose。

en: (of time) To take or use.
usage: be occupied with 忙于；occupied 被占用的。

en: To attempt to stop the progression of; to resist or antagonize by physical means, or by arguments, etc.; to contend against.

examples.en: There is still time to oppose this plan.
Many religious leaders oppose cloning humans.
They are opposed to any form of hierarchy.

usage: oppose doing 反对做；opposite 相反的；反义词 support。

en: To join in, to take part, to involve oneself (in something).
usage: participate in 参加；participation 参与。

en: To become aware of, through the physical senses or by thinking; to see; to understand.
usage: perceive...as 把……视为；perception 感知。

en: (NNES or European Union documents) To make or render precise; to specify.
usage: precisely 精确地；precision 精确度。

en: A sweet spread made of any of a variety of fruits.

examples.en: Every people have the right to preserve its identity and culture.
to preserve peaches or grapes
to preserve appearances; to preserve silence

usage: preserve the environment 保护环境；preservation 保护。

en: To raise (someone) to a more important, responsible, or remunerative job or rank.

examples.en: He promoted his clerk to office manager.
They promoted the abolition of daylight saving time.
At the end of the season, three teams are promoted to the Premier League.
Having crossed the chessboard, his pawn was promoted to a queen.

usage: promote peace 促进和平；promotion 晋升/促销。
